lot_dimensions.min_lot_width_note: Top dimension chain reads: 'A' (0.60, west overhang beyond 1) | 1-2 (4.00) | 2-3 (2.00) | 3-4 (3.00) | 'A' (0.50, east overhang beyond 4). Sub-bracket '9.00' spans 1-4 (4.00+2.00+3.00=9.00), matching this house's grid master x_lines (1=0.0,2=4.0,3=6.0,4=9.0) exactly. However 0.60+4.00+2.00+3.00+0.50 = 10.10, NOT the printed header total of 14.10 -- a 4.00m unreconciled gap between the printed headline figure and the sum of its own dimension chain. Read as printed rather than silently corrected; flagged for human zoom review since this is a plain arithmetic mismatch within a single sheet, not a cross-sheet discrepancy.
lot_dimensions.min_lot_depth_note: Left-side vertical dimension chain (top to bottom): 1.00 (above grid F, undimensioned-elsewhere zone containing ดินถม) | F-E (2.00) | E-D (3.70) | D-C (2.20) | C-B (3.70) | B-A (1.80) | 3.00 (below grid A, before the road). A separate bracket labeled '13.40' spans F-A (2.00+3.70+2.20+3.70+1.80=13.40), matching this house's grid master y_lines (F=13.40,E=11.40,D=7.70,C=5.50,B=1.80,A=0.0) exactly. But 1.00+13.40+3.00 = 17.40, NOT the printed header total of 19.40 -- a 2.00m unreconciled gap, same kind of finding as the width gap above. Read as printed rather than guessed/corrected.

B = ระยะปรับตามขนาดที่ดิน แต่ไม่น้อยกว่า 3 ม. หรือตามที่กฎหมายกำหนด

warnings: This sheet (A-03) is the site-placement plan, distinct from the room-level floor plan (A-04, หน้า06) -- same ground-floor building outline shown at 1:100, but this sheet's own content is the lot boundary, setback labels A/B, grading notes, and road, hence room-level detail is deliberately not repeated in as much depth here.

GRID-RELEVANT DUMMY-GRID CROSS-CHECK (reporting only, master file not edited): the grid master (หน้า00_gridline.json) flags a provisional dummy grid line 'Aprime' at pos_m=-2.05 (south of named grid A), sourced from real beams B1/B2 on the structural floor-1 beam plan (S-04), spanning 1-2, described there as 'a possible carport/terrace extension.' This site plan DOES show a matching feature: an unlabeled elevated slab/room at level '+0.20' in a ~3.00m-deep zone south of grid row B (between the ปรับระดับดินถม/เฉลียง row and the road), immediately south of/below the building's main grid extent, followed further south by a hatched '±0.00' grading zone before reaching the road. The '+0.20' level matches the จอดรถ (carport) level exactly, supporting the interpretation that this is a carport/entrance-apron slab extension consistent with the structural dummy grid finding. HOWEVER: (a) this site plan's zone is measured relative to grid row B, not directly grid row A as the structural note specifies (though B is only 1.80m north of A, so the two are in the same general area); (b) the site plan's 3.00m depth does not exactly match the structural dummy grid's 2.05m offset; (c) the exact x-position (1-2 vs 2-3 vs 3-4) of this unlabeled slab was not confidently pinpointed at this image resolution -- it visually appears to align more with the จอดรถ/เฉลียง column (east side) than the west 1-2 column the structural note specifies. This is therefore a PARTIAL, not fully conclusive, cross-confirmation -- flagged for human review to reconcile the exact grid-cell alignment between the architecture site plan and the S-04 structural beam plan.

Both the top width-dimension header (14.10m) and the left depth-dimension header (19.40m) fail to arithmetically reconcile against their own printed sub-dimension chains (gaps of 4.00m and 2.00m respectively) -- read and reported as printed, not corrected or guessed at, per the project's 'never invent values' rule. Similar unreconciled-dimension issues have been seen on prior sister houses' equivalent A-03 sheets.

Setback label 'A' (per the หมายเหตุ legend, 'ระยะปรับตามขนาดที่ดิน แต่ไม่น้อยกว่า 2 ม.') is printed at both ends of the TOP width dimension line (flanking 1 and 4) -- this is a different use of the letter 'A' than the grid ROW named 'A' at the bottom of the vertical grid lettering (F,E,D,C,B,A) -- a coincidental symbol reuse in the source drawing, not a data error, but worth flagging to avoid confusing the two 'A's when cross-referencing.

Notes wording (setback rule text, A/B definitions) matches houses #1-#4's equivalent A-03 sheet almost exactly (same government program template) aside from house-specific lot dimensions and room layout.

This house's grid uses letters F(top)-E-D-C-B-A(bottom) on the y-axis (6 named rows) and numbers 1-4 on the x-axis (4 named columns) -- a larger/different grid footprint than house #4's E-D-C-B-A (5 rows) x 1-2-3 (3 columns), consistent with the grid master's note that this house (บ้านครอบครัวไทยร่วมสมัย 5) has a larger floor area than houses #2-#4.
